gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Analyze a document's source through the text → `Node` pipeline and return its LSP diagnostics"] # [doc = " (JSON). `parse` failure → one ranged syntax diagnostic; `check_nodule` failure → one"] # [doc = " function-located type diagnostic; a clean nodule → no diagnostics."] # [must_use] pub fn source_diagnostics (text : & str) -> Vec < Value > { match parse (text) { Err (pe) => vec ! gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Like [`source_diagnostics`], but **isolating an internal analysis panic** as a structured"] # [doc = " diagnostic instead of letting it unwind through the server loop and kill the session."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " The parse → check pipeline returns explicit `Result`s for *expected* failures (syntax / type"] # [doc = " errors), and those flow through [`source_diagnostics`] untouched. This wrapper guards only the"] # [doc = " *unexpected* case: a bug in the checker/elaborator that fires one of its internal invariant"] # [doc = " `expect`s. Per **RFC-0013 I1** (a diagnostic is *additive*, never *substitutive*, and a refusal"] # [doc = " is **never swallowed and never silent**), such a panic must be **surfaced** — turned into a"] # [doc = " visible `internal`-coded diagnostic — not dropped (`logger.catch`-style swallowing, RFC-0013"] # [doc = " §4.5 X3) and not allowed to crash every open document. The server stays alive on the next edit."] # [must_use] pub fn resilient_source_diagnostics (text : & str) -> Vec < Value > { catching (move | | source_diagnostics (text)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Run `f`, converting a panic into a single surfaced `internal` diagnostic (RFC-0013 I1). Factored"] # [doc = " out so the isolation itself is unit-testable with a deliberately panicking closure (the"] # [doc = " mutant-witness: a server that let the panic propagate would crash here instead of reporting)."] fn catching < F > (f : F) -> Vec < Value > where F : FnOnce () -> Vec < Value > + std :: panic :: UnwindSafe , { match std :: panic :: catch_unwind (f) { Ok (diags) => diags , Err (payload) => vec ! gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A zero-located `internal`-coded diagnostic carrying a caught analysis panic. The range is the"] # [doc = " honest zero placeholder (an internal failure has no source span to point at — never a fabricated"] # [doc = " location); the message is explicit that the failure was **surfaced, not swallowed** (RFC-0013 I1)."] fn internal_error_diagnostic (detail : & str) -> Value { json ! ({ "range" : point_range (0 , 0 , 1) , "severity" : 1 , "code" : "internal" , "source" : SERVER_NAME , "message" : format ! ("internal analysis error (surfaced, not swallowed — RFC-0013 I1): {detail}") , }) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A ranged syntax diagnostic from a [`ParseError`] (the lexer's `line:col` is 1-based; LSP is"] # [doc = " 0-based — never a fabricated location, the position is real)."] fn parse_error_diagnostic (pe : & ParseError) -> Value { let line0 = pe . pos . line . saturating_sub (1) ; let col0 = pe . pos . col . saturating_sub (1) ; json ! gaps.snippet: # [doc = " A best-effort-located type diagnostic from a [`CheckError`]. The checker tracks the failing"] # [doc = " *function* (`site`), not yet the failing sub-expression span, so we locate `fn <site>` in the"] # [doc = " source for a usable range and carry the function name as the navigable breadcrumb; if the name is"] # [doc = " not found textually, we fall back to a zero range (honest, never a fabricated line)."] fn check_error_diagnostic (text : & str , ce : & CheckError) -> Value { let range = locate_fn (text , & ce . site) . map_or_else (| | point_range (0 , 0 , 1) , | (line0 , col0) | point_range (line0 , col0 , ce . site . len () as u32) ,) ; json ! ({ "range" : range , "severity" : 1 , "code" : "check" , "source" : SERVER_NAME , "message" : ce . message , "data" : { "breadcrumb" : [ce . site] } , }) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Locate a `fn <name>` declaration's name position (0-based `line, col`) in `text`, if present."] fn locate_fn (text : & str , name : & str) -> Option < (u32 , u32) > { let needle = format ! ("fn {name}") ; for (li , line) in text . lines () . enumerate () { if let Some (idx) = line . find (& needle) { let col = idx + "fn " . len () ; return Some ((li as u32 , col as u32)) ; } } None }
